title: Sinners Breaks Major Oscar Records with Best Actor & Cinematography Wins
text: Michael B. Jordan won the Best Actor Oscar on March 15, 2026, for his role in Sinners, where he played identical twins, Smoke and Stack. Michael B. Jordan's Best Actor win on his very first Oscar nomination made him the first performer in Oscar history to win Best Actor for a dual role in the same film. The Ryan Coogler-directed movie set its vampire horror story in the Jim Crow South, delivering a bold, genre-defying movie, brilliantly anchored by an electric performance from Michael B. Jordan. The Michael B. Jordan performance is plenty showy—he's playing twins and he's great at it and he gives them distinct personalities where you can tell them apart. Cinematography Recognition: Sinners also won Oscar statuettes for Best Original Score, Best Original Screenplay, and Best Cinematography. Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw's work in this film is jaw dropping. Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman in Oscar history to win Best Cinematography. Overall Film Reception: The film looks gorgeous, is beautifully acted, is fun, and exciting. Sinners performed strongly overall at the ceremony, earning 16 nominations.
